channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Trump threatens Iran after Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's funeral saw calls for his killing
item.description: Trump made the comments on his Truth Social after U.S. officials demanded that Iran make a public statement saying the Strait of Hormuz is open.
item.content:encoded:
Trump made the comments on his Truth Social after U.S. officials demanded that Iran make a public statement saying the Strait of Hormuz is open.

(Image credit: Iranian Supreme Leader Office)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: U.S.-Iran fighting appears to pause. And, life inside Israel's military zones in Gaza
item.description: Fighting between the U.S. and Iran appears to have paused after two days of clashing amid a shaky ceasefire. And, a look at what life is like inside Israel's expanding zone of control in Gaza.
item.content:encoded:
Fighting between the U.S. and Iran appears to have paused after two days of clashing amid a shaky ceasefire. And, a look at what life is like inside Israel's expanding zone of control in Gaza.

(Image credit: Majid Saeedi)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Count Binface: The intergalactic warrior who could upend Britain's strangest election
item.description: Meet Count Binface: the challenger from another planet taking on Nigel Farage as questions over the Reform UK leader's finances overshadow his election comeback.
item.content:encoded:
Meet Count Binface: the challenger from another planet taking on Nigel Farage as questions over the Reform UK leader's finances overshadow his election comeback.

(Image credit: Scott A Garfitt)
item.dc:creator: Fatima Al-Kassab

item.title: 9 months into a ceasefire, Israel now controls nearly 70% of Gaza
item.description: When the U.S. brokered a ceasefire last year, Israel controlled half of Gaza. Now Israeli forces have pushed deeper, and Palestinians are paying a deadly price.
item.content:encoded:
When the U.S. brokered a ceasefire last year, Israel controlled half of Gaza. Now Israeli forces have pushed deeper, and Palestinians are paying a deadly price.

(Image credit: Anas Baba)
item.dc:creator: Anas Baba

item.title: One of Spain's deadliest wildfires kills at least 12 people, with 23 others missing
item.description: Several victims of the fire in the southern province of Almeria, a popular holiday destination, were found inside burnt-out vehicles and were thought to have died while trying to flee the flames.
item.content:encoded:
Several victims of the fire in the southern province of Almeria, a popular holiday destination, were found inside burnt-out vehicles and were thought to have died while trying to flee the flames.

(Image credit: INFOCA)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Ebola death toll reaches 600, as new cases suspected in other parts of Congo
item.description: New suspected cases of Ebola have been reported in parts of Congo that were previously unaffected, the government said, signaling the continued spread of the disease beyond the epicenter in Ituri.
item.content:encoded:
New suspected cases of Ebola have been reported in parts of Congo that were previously unaffected, the government said, signaling the continued spread of the disease beyond the epicenter in Ituri.

(Image credit: Dirole Lotsima Dieudonne)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: U.S. and Iran exchange intensifying fire across Mideast, threatening ceasefire deal
item.description: Back-and-forth attacks have repeatedly threatened the ceasefire, but Thursday's appeared bigger all around.
item.content:encoded:
Back-and-forth attacks have repeatedly threatened the ceasefire, but Thursday's appeared bigger all around.

(Image credit: Hasan Salavati)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Former U.S. envoy reacts to Iran strikes and NATO summit
item.description: NPR's Scott Detrow speaks with former U.S. Ambassador to NATO Nicholas Burns regarding this week's developments in the U.S.-Iran conflict -- and the NATO summit this week.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Scott Detrow speaks with former U.S. Ambassador to NATO Nicholas Burns regarding this week's developments in the U.S.-Iran conflict -- and the NATO summit this week.
item.dc:creator: Kathryn Fink

item.title: Where are Iran and Ukraine wars headed after NATO summit?
item.description: Fresh strikes between Iran and the United States and a tense ceasefire scrapped — Is the war with Iran back on?
item.content:encoded: Fresh strikes between Iran and the United States and a tense ceasefire scrapped — Is the war with Iran back on?
item.dc:creator: Mary Louise Kelly

item.title: Graham Platner ends Senate bid. And, why Nolan Wells' death captured national attention
item.description: Democrat Graham Platner ended his bid last night for U.S. Senate. And, prominent civil rights attorney Ben Crump has been enlisted to help get answers in the July Fourth weekend death of Nolan Wells.
item.content:encoded:
Democrat Graham Platner ended his bid last night for U.S. Senate. And, prominent civil rights attorney Ben Crump has been enlisted to help get answers in the July Fourth weekend death of Nolan Wells.

(Image credit: Laura Brett)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton
